identity.philosophy: Function over form, but form must be aggressive. We are building a tool for truth, not a toy. The interface should feel like a 'heads-up display' (HUD) for your digital soul.
principles: Fusion over Imitation: Mix Terminal aesthetics with modern SaaS usability.
Emotion First: The user should feel 'plugged in' and 'secure'.
Content is King: Data is the hero. Metadata is the story.
Negative Space: Use it to group logical data sets.
Contrast: High contrast for readability in low-light environments.

typography.installation: Add this to index.html or import in CSS: @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500&family=Rajdhani:wght@500;600;700&display=swap');

usage.h1: font-heading font-bold text-4xl md:text-6xl tracking-tight uppercase
usage.h2: font-heading font-semibold text-2xl md:text-3xl tracking-wide text-primary/90
usage.label: font-mono text-xs uppercase tracking-widest text-zinc-500

Card.base: bg-[#0A0A0A] border border-zinc-800 rounded-sm relative overflow-hidden

Badge.base: font-mono text-[10px] px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full uppercase tracking-wider border

Progress.root: h-2 bg-zinc-900 rounded-full overflow-hidden border border-zinc-800

visual_enhancers.scanlines: background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255,255,255,0), rgba(255,255,255,0) 50%, rgba(0,0,0,0.1) 50%, rgba(0,0,0,0.1)); background-size: 100% 4px;

assets.icons: Use Lucide React icons. Import specific icons like 'Shield', 'Globe', 'HardDrive', 'Cpu', 'Eye', 'Lock', 'Unlock', 'Activity', 'Terminal'.
assets.charts: Use Recharts within Shadcn Chart component.

universal_guidelines: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: 'transition: all'. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add '.App { text-align: center; }' in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡 for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute.
implementation_steps: STEP 1: Setup Global Styles. Update index.css with the provided font imports and color variables (if utilizing CSS variables). Ensure 'dark' class is applied to html/body.
STEP 2: Build Layout Shell. Create a Sidebar + Main Content layout. Sidebar should be collapsible on mobile.
STEP 3: Create Core Components. Customize Shadcn Card, Button, Badge to match the 'Cyberpunk/HUD' aesthetic defined in 'components' section.
STEP 4: Implement Dashboard View. Build the Bento Grid layout. Create 'Widget' components for each metadata category (Browser, Device, Network, etc.).
STEP 5: Add Logic. Hook up the metadata extraction logic (navigator object, etc.) to the widgets.
STEP 6: Add Export & Details. Create the Export Modal and detailed view for each category.
